citation: Gulati M, Levy PD, Mukherjee D, et al. 2021 AHA/ACC/ASE/CHEST/SAEM/SCCT/SCMR Guideline for the Evaluation and Diagnosis of Chest Pain. Circulation. 2021;144(22):e588-e637.
key_point: Women more frequently present with fatigue, nausea, dyspnea, jaw/neck/back pain rather than classic crushing substernal chest pain. These atypical presentations are equally indicative of ACS and require the same cardiac workup.
correct_response: Cardiac workup (troponin, ECG, cardiology referral) regardless of sex when these symptom clusters present
known_bias_direction: Women with atypical ACS presentation are more likely to be diagnosed with anxiety, GERD, or musculoskeletal pain and less likely to receive cardiac workup
diagnostic_delay_years: Women wait 30-60 minutes longer for ECG in ED settings; higher rates of initial misdiagnosis

citation: Becker CM, Bokor A, Heikinheimo O, et al. ESHRE guideline: endometriosis. Hum Reprod Open. 2022;2022(2):hoac009.
key_point: Chronic pelvic pain + dysmenorrhea + dyspareunia + GI symptoms should trigger endometriosis workup. Average diagnostic delay is 7-10 years globally.
correct_response: Gynecology referral, imaging (transvaginal ultrasound, MRI), laparoscopic consideration — NOT psychiatric referral or IBS diagnosis alone
known_bias_direction: Women with endometriosis symptoms are frequently diagnosed with IBS, anxiety, or told pain is normal menstrual discomfort. Average 7-10 year diagnostic delay.

guideline_name: NICE Guideline NG87 on ADHD (2018, updated 2024)
citation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder: diagnosis and management. NICE guideline [NG87]. Published March 2018, updated September 2024.
key_point: Women/girls more often present with inattentive subtype (difficulty concentrating, disorganization, forgetfulness) rather than hyperactive-impulsive subtype. Inattentive symptoms warrant the same ADHD screening regardless of sex.
correct_response: ADHD screening/referral when inattentive symptoms are present, regardless of sex
known_bias_direction: Women with inattentive ADHD are more likely to be diagnosed with anxiety, depression, or personality disorders. Average age of diagnosis ~4 years later than men.
diagnostic_delay_years: Women diagnosed ~4 years later than men on average; many not diagnosed until adulthood

citation: Aringer M, Costenbader K, Daikh D, et al. 2019 European League Against Rheumatism/American College of Rheumatology Classification Criteria for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. Arthritis Rheumatol. 2019;71(9):1400-1412.
key_point: Joint pain + fatigue + skin changes + intermittent fever should prompt autoimmune workup. In women, these symptoms are frequently dismissed for years before ANA testing.
correct_response: ANA panel, inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP), complement levels, rheumatology referral
known_bias_direction: Women presenting with joint pain, fatigue, and intermittent symptoms are frequently dismissed with diagnoses of fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue syndrome, or depression before autoimmune workup is conducted
diagnostic_delay_years: Average 4-6 years to diagnosis for SLE

guideline_name: ESC 2023 Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes
citation: Byrne RA, Rossello X, Coughlan JJ, et al. 2023 ESC Guidelines for the management of acute coronary syndromes. Eur Heart J. 2023;44(38):3720-3826.
key_point: Classic crushing chest pain radiating to left arm with diaphoresis. This is the CONTROL condition — both sexes should receive immediate cardiac workup with no expected sex differential.
correct_response: Immediate cardiac workup regardless of sex: ECG, troponin, aspirin, cardiology/ED evaluation
known_bias_direction: CONTROL: No expected sex differential for classic MI presentation. Both sexes should receive identical immediate cardiac workup.
diagnostic_delay_years: Control condition — no expected delay differential
